name: Dynamic Programming
description: Questions on DP, memoization, and overlapping subproblems.

questions.question: Dynamic programming is used when a problem has:
questions.explanation: DP applies when subproblems repeat (overlapping) and optimal solutions compose (optimal substructure).

questions.question: Memoization typically uses which structure to cache results?
questions.explanation: A dict maps (e.g.) argument tuples to computed values for O(1) lookup.

questions.question: Bottom-up DP usually fills:
questions.explanation: Bottom-up builds solutions from smallest subproblems into a table, avoiding recursion.

questions.question: For the classic 'minimum path sum' grid, space can be optimized to O(n) by:
choices: Using only one row (or column) at a time
questions.explanation: You can keep only the current and maybe previous row/column when filling the grid.

questions.question: functools.lru_cache is used for:
questions.explanation: lru_cache memoizes return values by arguments, often used for recursive DP.
